subject: PATIENT
heading: Result Date: [ENCOUNTER_DATE]__page_no__3
text: result date: [encounter_date] procedure: xr hip. clinical indication: pain technique: ap and frog lateral views of the right hip were performed. comparison: findings: there is normal mineralization and alignment. no fracture or osseous lesion is identified. there are no significant degenerative changes in the hip. the soft tissues are unremarkable. there is vascular calcification noted. rptat: aa impression - no gross acute fracture noted. limited visualization of the right femoral neck due to patient positioning. if there is a high suspicion for possible fracture, mri is recommended. dictated by: [provider_name] on [encounter_date] 18:39 electronically signed by: [provider_name] on [encounter_date] 18:39 mri brain wo contrast result date: [encounter_date] procedure: mr brain without contrast. clinical indication: neurologic deficit. technique: multiplanar, multisequence, mr imaging of the brain was performed without contrast. comparison: ct stroke code imaging performed [encounter_date]. findings: there is no focus of parenchymal diffusion restriction to suggest acute infarction. there are mild subcortical and periventricular foci of white matter t2/flair hyperintensity; these are nonspecific but most likely reflect chronic microvascular white matter ischemic changes. there is no acute intracranial hemorrhage. there is no extra-axial fluid collection. overall, sulcal and cisternal spaces are preserved. there is no hydrocephalus, midline shift, or significant mass effect. there is mild sulcal and ventricular prominence most suggestive of global cerebral parenchymal volume loss. there is no destructive osseous lesion. there has been prior bilateral cataract surgery. the visualized paranasal sinuses are clear. impression - 1. there is no acute infarction, acute intracranial hemorrhage, hydrocephalus, or midline shift. 2. there are mild chronic microvascular white matter ischemic changes with mild global cerebral parenchymal volume loss. further findings as detailed above. rptat: bbcc dictated by: [provider_name] on [encounter_date] 14:07

text: result date: [encounter_date] procedure: xr hip. clinical indication: pain technique: ap and frog lateral views of the right hip were performed. comparison: findings: there is normal mineralization and alignment. no fracture or osseous lesion is identified. there are no significant degenerative changes in the hip. the soft tissues are unremarkable. there is vascular calcification noted. rptat: aa impression - no gross acute fracture noted. limited visualization of the right femoral neck due to patient positioning. if there is a high suspicion for possible fracture, mri is recommended. dictated by: [provider_name] on [encounter_date] 18:39 electronically signed by: [provider_name] on [encounter_date] 18:39 mri brain wo contrast result date: [encounter_date] procedure: mr brain without contrast. clinical indication: neurologic deficit. technique: multiplanar, multisequence, mr imaging of the brain was performed without contrast. comparison: ct stroke code imaging performed [encounter_date]. findings: there is no focus of parenchymal diffusion restriction to suggest acute infarction. there are mild subcortical and periventricular foci of white matter t2/flair hyperintensity; these are nonspecific but most likely reflect chronic microvascular white matter ischemic changes. there is no acute intracranial hemorrhage. there is no extra-axial fluid collection. overall, sulcal and cisternal spaces are preserved. there is no hydrocephalus, midline shift, or significant mass effect. there is mild sulcal and ventricular prominence most suggestive of global cerebral parenchymal volume loss. there is no destructive osseous lesion. there has been prior bilateral cataract surgery. the visualized paranasal sinuses are clear. impression - 1. there is no acute infarction, acute intracranial hemorrhage, hydrocephalus, or midline shift. 2. there are mild chronic microvascular white matter ischemic changes with mild global cerebral parenchymal volume loss. further findings as detailed above. rptat: bbcc dictated by: [provider_name] on [encounter_date] 14:07

subject: PATIENT
heading: HOSPITAL COURSE:__page_no__8
text: hospital course: patient presented with altered level of consciousness was admitted to the hospital underwent neurowork-up including mri seen by consultation by neurology and nephrology. patient additionally had hemodialysis while in the hospital. patient diarrheal illness improved. patient clinically has been better. patient coincidently found to have a anterior communicating artery aneurysm which seen by neurointervention and no further treatment recommended. advised patient and family to avoid driving given his multiple conditions.

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: 2. Post Hospital Visit__page_no__13
text: history of present illness 2. post hospital visit verbal consent obtained for this video call for the purpose of this post snf/hosp assessment,. pt was admitted at [facility_name] on [encounter_date] for acute on chronic diastolic chf. presented with sob after dialysis, left subcostal chest pain. he was admitted prior to this hospitalization for substernal non radiating cp. ekg showed rbbb. he was evaluated and cleared for dc by cardio. during this admission on [encounter_date] he was stable but required 21 of o2 admitted for fluid overload.. he was found to have appendicitis with perforation and abscess. he was also found to have a kidney mass. no surgical intervention done for the appendicitis, was administered with zosyn and dcd with augmentin x1 week with probiotics, r renal mass confirmed by mri suspicion of renal cell carcinoma, evaluated by oncology/urology = discussed with family members- for tx plan- conservative mgt with regular monitoring of the size vs nephrectomy. discussiion will c/w oncology/urology outpt.. severe protein malnutrition- started on marinol to increase appetite. fluid overload/chf was - fluids were managed by hd/resolving sob. chest pain- likely misinterpreted from his left abdominal pain/not chest. cadiologhgy evaluated- no further ischemic workup done. pt was transferred to [facility_name] for further tx and rehab. has allzheimers dementia, pakinsons, bph, tla, htn hd on hd 3x week (tts). he was dcd from snf on [encounter_date]. son reuested for home therqapy/was started. 2x week. he is dependent on wife/family for adls. wheelchair bound. non diabetic. dm screebed [encounter_date] - a1c 4.4%.

text: history of present illness 2. post hospital visit verbal consent obtained for this video call for the purpose of this post snf/hosp assessment,. pt was admitted at [facility_name] on [encounter_date] for acute on chronic diastolic chf. presented with sob after dialysis, left subcostal chest pain. he was admitted prior to this hospitalization for substernal non radiating cp. ekg showed rbbb. he was evaluated and cleared for dc by cardio. during this admission on [encounter_date] he was stable but required 21 of o2 admitted for fluid overload.. he was found to have appendicitis with perforation and abscess. he was also found to have a kidney mass. no surgical intervention done for the appendicitis, was administered with zosyn and dcd with augmentin x1 week with probiotics, r renal mass confirmed by mri suspicion of renal cell carcinoma, evaluated by oncology/urology = discussed with family members- for tx plan- conservative mgt with regular monitoring of the size vs nephrectomy. discussiion will c/w oncology/urology outpt.. severe protein malnutrition- started on marinol to increase appetite. fluid overload/chf was - fluids were managed by hd/resolving sob. chest pain- likely misinterpreted from his left abdominal pain/not chest. cadiologhgy evaluated- no further ischemic workup done. pt was transferred to [facility_name] for further tx and rehab. has allzheimers dementia, pakinsons, bph, tla, htn hd on hd 3x week (tts). he was dcd from snf on [encounter_date]. son reuested for home therqapy/was started. 2x week. he is dependent on wife/family for adls. wheelchair bound. non diabetic. dm screebed [encounter_date] - a1c 4.4%.

text: history of present illness 2. post hospital visit verbal consent obtained for this video call for the purpose of this post snf/hosp assessment,. pt was admitted at [facility_name] on [encounter_date] for acute on chronic diastolic chf. presented with sob after dialysis, left subcostal chest pain. he was admitted prior to this hospitalization for substernal non radiating cp. ekg showed rbbb. he was evaluated and cleared for dc by cardio. during this admission on [encounter_date] he was stable but required 21 of o2 admitted for fluid overload.. he was found to have appendicitis with perforation and abscess. he was also found to have a kidney mass. no surgical intervention done for the appendicitis, was administered with zosyn and dcd with augmentin x1 week with probiotics, r renal mass confirmed by mri suspicion of renal cell carcinoma, evaluated by oncology/urology = discussed with family members- for tx plan- conservative mgt with regular monitoring of the size vs nephrectomy. discussiion will c/w oncology/urology outpt.. severe protein malnutrition- started on marinol to increase appetite. fluid overload/chf was - fluids were managed by hd/resolving sob. chest pain- likely misinterpreted from his left abdominal pain/not chest. cadiologhgy evaluated- no further ischemic workup done. pt was transferred to [facility_name] for further tx and rehab. has allzheimers dementia, pakinsons, bph, tla, htn hd on hd 3x week (tts). he was dcd from snf on [encounter_date]. son reuested for home therqapy/was started. 2x week. he is dependent on wife/family for adls. wheelchair bound. non diabetic. dm screebed [encounter_date] - a1c 4.4%.
